data.title: Yesterday, Grayscale GBTC had a net outflow of US$6.9 million and ETHE had a net outflow of US$3.8 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Farside Investors data shows that Grayscale GBTC had a net outflow of $6.9 million yesterday; ETHE had a net outflow of $3.8 million; the net inflow/outflow of the two mini ETFs was 0; in addition, Franklin's EZBC had a net outflow of $8.4 million.

data.title: Bridgewater reduced its holdings in 6 technology giants in the fourth quarter and built a position in Tesla
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Bridgewater Fund's updated fourth quarter 13F holdings file shows that its total holdings at the end of the fourth quarter were $21.8 billion, an increase of $4.1 billion from the third quarter. In terms of reduction, it sold 1.25 million shares of Nvidia (NVDA.O) and 750,000 shares of Google A in the fourth quarter, and also reduced its holdings of Meta, Microsoft, Apple and Amazon. Among them, Apple's reduction was the largest, reaching 40% or 410,000 shares; in terms of increase, Bridgewater bought nearly 7.4 million shares of S&P 500ETF-SPDR, an increase of 883%, and also increased its holdings of AMD, PayPal, eBay, Robinhood and Qualcomm. It is worth mentioning that Bridgewater also built a position in Tesla (TSLA.O) with 150,000 shares.

data.title: Zoom Urged To Adopt Bitcoin Strategy For Growth
data.description: According to Odaily, Eric Semler, founder of Semler Scientific, suggested on the X platform that Zoom Communications (ZM) should adopt a Bitcoin strategy to overcome growth stagnation and enhance shareholder value. Semler described Zoom as being in a 'zombie zone' and in urgent need of new growth drivers. He highlighted that Zoom has $7.7 billion in cash reserves, an annual free cash flow of $2 billion, and access to low-cost financing. By entering the Bitcoin space, Zoom could quickly become one of the largest corporate Bitcoin holders globally. Semler cited the example of SMLR, which adopted Bitcoin as a corporate asset in mid-2024, following the MicroStrategy (MSTR) model, and accumulated 3,192 BTC, valued at approximately $305 million. Since disclosing its Bitcoin holdings, SMLR's stock price has doubled.

data.title: Bitwise Experiences Significant Net Outflow
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from Farside Investors reveals that Bitwise's BITB experienced a net outflow of $15.7 million on February 14.

data.title: Whales accumulate $3.8B in Bitcoin during the recent dip, with a net inflow of 40K BTC on Feb 5
data.description:
Large market participants in the crypto industry seem to have capitalized on the recent market dip by snapping up Bitcoin at discounted prices. On-chain data supports the claim, showing that the large players received massive inflows on February 5th.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/whales-accumulate-3-8b-in-bitcoin/

data.title: Vitalik Buterin praises compliance-focused privacy project Railgun for preventing zKLend attacker from laundering stolen funds
data.description:

source: https://www.theblock.co/post/340807/vitalik-buterin-praises-compliance-focused-privacy-project-railgun-for-preventing-zklend-attacker-from-laundering-stolen-funds?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss
